DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCE: ROLANDO PASQUALI. ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF MARIANNA P'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SETS 2 AND 3, IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. HOWEVER, THE COURT NOTES THAT WHILE THE RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE, IT IS NOT LIKELY THAT DEFENDANT WILL BE ABLE TO RESPOND FURTHER WHILE HE IS INCARCERATED. NEITHER IS IT LIKELY THAT THE COURT WOULD ISSUE A TERMINATING SANCTION AS THE DISCOVERY THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION DO NOT GO TO LIABILITY. THE COURT FINDS CIRCUMSTANCES WHICH MAKE THE IMPOSITION OF MONETARY SANCTIONS UNJUST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: ROLANDO PASQUALI, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, ALYSSA P. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF ALYSSA P'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET 4, IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $463.80, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND TO ENFORCE SUBPOENAS OVER WRITTEN OBJECTIONS IS GRANTED. THE COURT ORDERS MARIE PIAZZA AND SHARON OWENS TO RE-APPEAR AT DEPOSITION AT THE OFFICE OF COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WITHIN 7 DAYS TO ANSWER EACH OF THE QUESTIONS LISTED IN PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF MATTERS IN DISPUTE, TOGETHER WITH REASONABLY RELATED FOLLOW-UP QUESTIONS. IN ADDITION, THE OBJECTIONS TO THE SUBPOENAS SPECIFIED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT ORDERS COUNSEL FOR DEPONENTS, AND MARIE PIAZZA AND SHARON OWENS TO JOINTLY PAY THE SUM OF $1746.30 BY WAY OF COSTS AND SANCTIONS WITHIN 7 DAYS. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE OBJECTIONS AND INSTRUCTIONS NOT TO ANSWER THE QUESTIONS AT DEPOSITION LACKED MERIT AND WERE MADE WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
